raw_consumer_statement:
On XXXX XXXX of XXXX  XXXX, I had an unfortunate experience where I allowed access to my bank and financial accounts. This included my emails and passwords. I was also scammed out of {$2000.00}. About XXXX XXXX ago, I received a warning from my credit card company that there may be fraud on my account. I told them there definitely was and they told me they would take care of it. They sent me a new card. 
About a month  later, I received a letter telling me that I was responsible for the charges and that they had proof it was me. I asked them what the proof was, and they told me my email was used so I was responsible. I then explained my hacking situation so we opened an additional dispute. These charges are over {$1000.00} for show tickets in XXXX. I live XXXX XXXX  away in XXXX  and have not been traveling at all. I have excellent credit and they have told me they will take me to collections if I do not pay this. I should absolutely not be held responsible for purchases I did not make. As I said, they notified me because it seemed suspicious.
